rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2024, line 1 2 - HEARING ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION Off calendar as duplicate entry. The petition and motion can be resolved on a single line. = (302/RBU)

Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2024, line 1, 1 - PETITIONER SHELBY BATES MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION Continued to October 3, 2024, on the court's motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA BOGAC-MOORE VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing On Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 And 583.250
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2024, line 14, DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 And 583.250 Defendant's motion to dismiss is denied. Defendant is estopped from relying on the three-year period to dismiss this action. (CCP 583.140; Tresway Aero, Inc. v. Superior Court (1971) 5 Cal.3d 431, 437-439 [doctrine of equitable estoppel is applicable to motions to dismiss for failure to effectuate service within three years].) *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON WEBBE VS. SONIA YVETTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2024, line 8, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER SMITH, SONIA SOLIS MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC. Sonia Solis and Andrew Smith's motion "for determination of good faith settlement" is denied without prejudice. Solis drove the middle vehicle in a three-vehicle chain-reaction collision on Highway 101 in San Francisco. Plaintiff settled with Solis and Smith (apparently the vehicle's owner) for $30,000 insurance policy limits. But the court is provided insufficient information on several other key factors in a good faith settlement analysis: e.g., rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery, settlor's proportionate liability, settlors' financial condition. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)
